Research: This paper reports on a phase 1-2 clinical trial of renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel), an investigational CRISPR-Cas12a gene-edited autologous hematopoietic stem-cell therapy designed to treat transfusion-dependent β-thalassemia by disrupting BCL11A binding sites in the HBG1 and HBG2 promoters to reactivate fetal hemoglobin (HbF) production.

Research: ## Research Report: CRISPR-Cas12a Gene Editing of HBG1 and HBG2 Promoters to Treat β-Thalassemia
Research: Analysis of CRISPR-Cas12a Gene Editing of HBG1 and HBG2 Promoters to Treat β-Thalassemia.

Research: This phase 1-2 clinical trial investigated renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel), a CRISPR-Cas12a gene-edited autologous hematopoietic stem-cell therapy, in nine participants with transfusion-dependent β-thalassemia. The therapy targets BCL11A binding sites in HBG1 and HBG2 promoters to reactivate fetal hemoglobin (HbF) production. All participants achieved transfusion independence with rapid neutrophil engraftment and sustained hemoglobin levels >12 g/dL total and >11 g/dL fetal hemoglobin between months 6-18. Adverse events were generally consistent with myeloablative conditioning, with no unexpected safety signals.
